name: Romy's Kahuku Prawns
tagline: Farm-raised shrimp from their own ponds — the butter garlic are plumper than Giovanni's, fight me.
mustOrder: steamed garlic prawns (steaming preserves the sweetness better than frying)
parking: Dirt lot next to the shrimp ponds
talkStory: Founded by Romy and Amy Aquino, a Filipino-American family who started raising prawns in converted sugar cane irrigation ponds in the early 2000s. One of the only Kahuku shrimp spots where the prawns literally come from ponds on the same property — you can see them from where you eat. The steamed preparation (versus the heavy garlic butter at Giovanni's) lets the natural sweetness shine. Featured on Bizarre Foods with Andrew Zimmern. Cash only, small family operation — expect 30-90 minute waits and the prawns can sell out by mid-afternoon on busy days. Arrive before 11am for the shortest line.
